title: Lellingeria subimpressa
intro: Lellingeria subimpressa is a species of fern belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and skin irritations, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Lellingeria subimpressa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lellingeria subimpressa is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, or used in the form of a topical cream or ointment, to leverage its therapeutic properties.
